I don't have any specific past conversations about your phone's battery issues, but I can offer some well-rounded tips to help improve your phone's battery life! Here are some practical suggestions:

### 🔋 Extend Your Phone's Battery Life

**Screen & Display**
- **Lower screen brightness** or enable auto-brightness — the display is one of the biggest battery drains.
- **Reduce screen timeout** so the screen turns off sooner when idle.
- **Use dark mode** if your phone has an OLED/AMOLED screen, as dark pixels use less power.

**Connectivity**
- **Turn off Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and GPS** when you're not using them.
- **Disable mobile data** in areas with poor signal — your phone works harder to find a signal, draining the battery faster.
- **Enable Airplane Mode** in low-signal areas if you don't need connectivity.

**Apps & Background Activity**
- **Restrict background app refresh** — many apps drain battery by running in the background.
- **Check your battery usage stats** (Settings > Battery) to identify which apps are the biggest culprits.
- **Uninstall or disable apps** you rarely use.

**Settings & Features**
- **Enable Battery Saver / Low Power Mode** to automatically limit background activity.
- **Turn off push notifications** for non-essential apps.
- **Disable "Hey Siri" / "OK Google"** always-on listening if you don't use it.

**Charging Habits**
- Try to keep your battery **between 20–80%** rather than letting it drain to 0% or charging to 100% constantly — this helps preserve long-term battery health.
- **Avoid charging in extreme heat**, which degrades battery capacity over time.

**Portable Power Bank**
- If you're frequently running low, a **portable power bank** is a great backup. Compact options like the **Anker PowerCore 5000** or **Anker PowerCore Fusion** are reliable and easy to carry.

Great news — I can see from your past conversations that you're planning to **bike to work** and have already been enjoying podcasts during your bus commute (specifically *"How I Built This"*!). Here are some personalised activity suggestions tailored to you:

---

### 🎧 Audio-Based (Perfect for Cycling!)
- **Podcasts** – You're already loving *"How I Built This"*! Keep it up, or explore similar entrepreneurship/business podcasts like *Masters of Scale* or *The Tim Ferriss Show*.
- **Audiobooks** – Use apps like Audible or Scribd to work through books you've been meaning to read.
- **Language Learning** – Apps like Duolingo or Babbel have audio modes great for hands-free learning on the go.
- **News & Industry Updates** – Stay informed with a morning news podcast or industry-specific content.

---

### 🧠 Mental & Mindfulness
- **Mental Preparation** – Use the ride to mentally plan your day, prioritise your to-do list, and set intentions before arriving at the office.
- **Mindfulness/Meditation Audio** – Apps like Headspace or Calm have commute-friendly guided sessions.

---

### 🎵 Entertainment
- **Motivating Music Playlists** – Create an energising playlist to power your ride and set a positive mood for the day.

---

### 💡 Tips for Your Bike Commute Specifically
Since you're cycling, hands-free audio activities are your best bet. Avoid anything that requires looking at a screen for safety. A good pair of **bone conduction headphones** can keep you aware of traffic while still enjoying audio content.
